name: LLMService_call_llm_latency_under_threshold_with_stub_client
description: Performance test measuring latency of LLMService.call_llm when the underlying openai client returns quickly. Uses a stub openai client that immediately returns a shaped response. Ensures LLMResponse is returned and call duration is within acceptable threshold.
input: Instantiate LLMService with openai_client_factory returning a stub client where chat.completions.create is an async function returning an object shaped like: response.choices=[SimpleNamespace(message=SimpleNamespace(content='Generated summary...'))], response.usage=SimpleNamespace(prompt_tokens=10, completion_tokens=20, total_tokens=30). Call call_llm(system_prompt=SYSTEM_PROMPT, user_prompt='Please enroll me', context_chunks=['doc1','doc2'], llm_params={'temperature':0.1}).
output: An instance of LLMResponse is returned quickly (<200ms wall-clock in unit test environment) with text matching the stub content and tokens_used matching usage values.
success_criteria: response.tokens_used is a dict with keys 'prompt_tokens','completion_tokens','total_tokens' and integer values matching stub
call duration measured via time() is below the configured threshold (e.g., < 200 ms) in the test environment
error_scenarios: chat.completions.create raises or times out (should result in propagated exception)
test environment too slow causing false positives on latency assertion
